title: tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum
intro: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum, commonly known as the Red Pyrethrum or Carnation Pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species belonging to the sunflower family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic actions. The therapeutic actions of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents present in this plant include pyrethrins, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum has many benefits, such as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, as well as its antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infections and promote wound healing. This plant is also known to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and improve overall cardiovascular health. Additionally, its essential oils have been shown to possess analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle cramps, as well as promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. By incorporating Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, enhanced quality of life, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
actions: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its use in treating a variety of conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Its antiseptic and antifungal properties also help to soothe and heal minor cuts and scrapes, while its antioxidant properties may help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease. The therapeutic actions of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum can improve people's daily lives by reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting overall health and well-being, making it an effective natural remedy for a range of health issues. By harnessing the therapeutic properties of this plant, individuals can enjoy better physical and mental health, increased energy, and enhanced quality of life.
intro_study: According to "Natural product communications", Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum (also known as Tansy) does not pose a health risk when misidentified as Achillea grandifolia, and its absence of the toxic alpha-thujone may be considered a benefit.
constituents: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and terpenoids, which possess significant medicinal properties. These constituents include tanshinones, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and improve overall well-being. The sesquiterpenoids present in Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum, such as artemisinin and tanacetone, exhibit potent antimalarial and antifungal properties, respectively, making them valuable in the treatment of various infectious diseases. By harnessing these medicinal constituents, individuals can benefit from improved heart health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced protection against infectious diseases, ultimately leading to a better quality of life.
parts: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum contain sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The flowers of this plant contain sesquiterpene lactones, particularly camphor, which has been traditionally used as a rubefacient to relieve pain and reduce inflammation. The roots of Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which have been reported to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. carneum can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while topical application may lead to redness, itching, and blistering. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils can also result in headaches, dizziness, and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant's pyrethrin content may cause neurological symptoms, including tremors and seizures, especially in children and pregnant women.
